title: Placement Oriented Java Training
content: Event Date: 19th, 20th, 21st, 26th, 27th, 28th October 2024 Program Overview: The Training and Placement Team of School of Computer Applications and Technology, Galgotias University successfully organized a Placement-Oriented Java Training Program from 19th October 2024 to 28th October 2024. This program aimed to equip students with essential Java programming skills tailored to meet industry demands, enhancing their employability in top software companies. Lead Trainer and Mentors: The training sessions were conducted by Mr. Sharad Gupta, Assistant Professor, Galgotias University, who possesses extensive knowledge in Java programming and software development. His industry-focused teaching approach enabled students to grasp core concepts effectively and apply them to real-world scenarios. The program was supervised and supported by the Training & Placement team members, Dr. Pratima Singh and Mr. Rajesh Sharma, whose guidance was instrumental in organizing a well-structured and impactful training experience. Objectives of the Program: The primary objectives of the Placement-Oriented Java Training Program included: Building a strong foundation in core Java programming skills. Preparing students for technical interviews with industry-aligned Java knowledge. Providing hands-on experience through practical sessions on important Java topics. Enhancing the problem-solving capabilities of students using Java. Program Highlights and Benefits: The program was structured with a combination of theoretical sessions, practical coding exercises, and mock technical interviews, enabling students to: Build confidence in Java programming and understand its practical applications. Gain hands-on experience with coding challenges similar to those in placement exams. Develop a portfolio of projects that showcase their Java skills for potential employers. Impact on Student Placement: The Placement-Oriented Java Training Program received highly positive feedback from participants. It significantly enhanced their technical proficiency and readiness for campus placements in software companies. With knowledge of core and advanced Java concepts, students are now better prepared to excel in technical assessments, coding rounds, and interviews. Conclusion: This Java training program, conducted by Training & Placement Team of School of Computer Applications and Technology with the expertise of Mr. Sharad Gupta and supported by Dr. Pratima Singh and Mr. Rajesh Sharma, was an invaluable opportunity for students to acquire placement-focused Java skills. The team's initiative has paved the way for students to pursue rewarding careers in software engineering and related fields. Mentor Name – Prof. (Dr.) Vineeta Khemchandani Department Name – Department of Computer Applications and Technology
